clues: In the 2025 season, he rushed for 471 yards and 1 touchdown on 131 carries while adding 33 receptions for 186 yards across 11 games.
On Christmas Day 2020, he tied an NFL record set in 1929 by Ernie Nevers with six rushing touchdowns in a single game, finishing with 155 yards and helping clinch a division title.
College: Tennessee; Draft Year: 2017
He and linebacker Demario Davis were both named Second-Team All-Pro in the same season during their eight years as teammates, before Davis departed for the Jets.
He plays Running Back for the New Orleans Saints

accolades: 2019 NL Pitcher of the Month (August & September)
clues: In 2025, he posted a 10.5 K/9 rate while striking out 188 batters across 161 innings, though his ERA ballooned to 4.64.
Nationality: American; He was adopted at three weeks old by a single mother who is a senior director of corporate finance at NBCUniversal.

In the second half of 2019, he posted a 0.91 ERA over 15 starts with 124 strikeouts in 99.1 innings — the third-lowest second-half ERA in the modern era, behind only Jake Arrieta (2015) and Greg Maddux (1994).
He started Game 1 of the 2024 World Series alongside teammate Shohei Ohtani, who is no longer on his current team.
He plays Starting Pitcher for the Detroit Tigers
In the 2024-25 season, he averaged 24.7 points and 11.6 rebounds per game across 51 games split between two teams.
He is the first player in basketball history to win an NCAA championship, an NBA title, an Olympic gold medal, and a FIBA World Cup gold medal.
College: Kentucky; Draft Year: 2012
He won the 2020 NBA Championship alongside LeBron James, who is no longer on his current team.
He plays Power Forward/Center for the Washington Wizards
In the 2025 NFL season, he recorded 59 receptions for 850 yards and 6 touchdowns in 15 games before being suspended for the final two games.
At the 2019 NFL Combine, he ran a 4.33-second 40-yard dash at 228 pounds and posted a 40.5-inch vertical jump, one of the most dominant combine performances ever by a wide receiver.
College: Ole Miss; Draft Year: 2019
In 2020, he and Tyler Lockett became the first pair of Seahawks wide receivers to each surpass 1,000 receiving yards in the same season since 1995. Lockett is no longer on his current team.
He plays Wide Receiver for the Pittsburgh Steelers
In 2025, he missed the first 70 games with elbow injuries but still slugged 24 home runs with a .594 slugging percentage in just 77 games.

Nationality: American; He went by Mike for the first two years of his MLB career before reclaiming his birth name after a trip to Europe, where hearing similar Italian names inspired him to embrace his unique given name.
In 2017, he became the first player since 2001 to hit 59 home runs in a season, earning NL MVP honors as the sixth player in MLB history to reach that total.
He and Juan Soto powered the lineup to the 2024 World Series, combining for eleven postseason home runs before Soto left for the Mets on a record $765 million deal.
He plays Designated Hitter for the New York Yankees
In the 2024-25 season, he averaged 17.1 points and 4.8 rebounds per game across two teams after being traded midseason from Golden State to Miami.
In the 2022 NBA Finals, he averaged 18.3 points and 8.8 rebounds per game, including a 26-point, 13-rebound performance in Game 5 that put his team one win from the title.
College: Kansas; Draft Year: 2014
He and Klay Thompson were both starters on the 2022 championship team before Thompson departed for Dallas in free agency.

accolades: 2022 AP Defensive Player of the Year
clues: In the 2025 season, he recorded 2 sacks and 2 forced fumbles in just 3 games before a torn ACL ended his year.
In 2022, he led the NFL with 18.5 sacks, becoming the first member of his franchise to win AP Defensive Player of the Year in over 20 years.
College: Ohio State; Draft Year: 2019
He and Deebo Samuel were both drafted in 2019 and reached Super Bowl LIV together as rookies before Samuel was traded to Washington in 2025.
He plays Defensive End for the San Francisco 49ers
In 2025, he hit .267 with 31 home runs, 31 stolen bases, and 35 doubles across 160 games, becoming just the second shortstop with multiple 30-30 seasons.
Nationality: Puerto Rican; He is known as Mr. Smile and is the only current Latino MLB player with his own signature shoe from a major brand.
He is one of only two shortstops in MLB history to record multiple 30-30 seasons, accomplishing the feat in both 2023 and 2025.
He and Pete Alonso powered the 2024 postseason run to the NLCS, where he hit a series-clinching grand slam in the NLDS against Philadelphia.
He plays Shortstop for the New York Mets
In the 2024-25 season, he averaged 18.1 points and 9.6 rebounds per game while continuing to anchor one of the league's top defenses.
On March 10, 2026, he scored 83 points against Washington, the second-highest single-game total in NBA history behind Wilt Chamberlain's 100.
College: Kentucky; Draft Year: 2017
He and Jimmy Butler reached the 2020 NBA Finals together before Butler was traded to Golden State in February 2025.

In the 2025 season, he recorded 57 receptions for 628 yards and 7 touchdowns in 11 games while earning the highest PFF grade (90.8) among all tight ends.
